value.body:
## Problem
Ticket creation now requires canonical ticket types (`epic`, `feature`, `bug`, `task`), but issue update patches accept arbitrary `frontmatter.type` values. This lets API or renderer callers write legacy or invalid types after creation, bypassing the canonical type migration and leaving tickets in inconsistent state.

## Evidence
- `packages/contracts/src/schemas/Inputs.ts:183` requires `CreateIssueInput.type` to be `TicketTypeId`.
- `packages/contracts/src/schemas/Inputs.ts:205` defines `UpdateIssueInput.frontmatter` as an `UnknownRecord`, so `frontmatter.type` is not schema-validated.
- `packages/database/src/services/DatabaseService.ts:1191` merges `patch.frontmatter` directly into the stored ticket frontmatter.
- `packages/database/src/services/DatabaseService.ts:3839` only validates that the resulting type is a non-empty string.

## Expected
Updating a ticket type should reject missing, display-label, legacy, or unknown values unless the update path explicitly normalizes a supported alias.

## Suggested fix
Validate `patch.frontmatter.type` with the same canonical write rules used by create, or route updates through a typed type-change operation. Add API/database tests that PATCHing `frontmatter.type: "initiative"`, `"Issue"`, or `"not-a-type"` is rejected or normalized intentionally.

frontmatter.title: Issue update patches can write invalid ticket types

value.title: Issue update patches can write invalid ticket types
